questions.question: What can you say on the degree of workability for a slump value obtained as 85 mm?

explanations.a: Correct! A slump value of 85 mm falls in the Medium Workability range (50-100 mm).
explanations.b: Incorrect. Low Workability corresponds to a slump of 25-50 mm.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Very Low Workability corresponds to a slump of 0-25 mm.
explanations.d: Incorrect. High Workability corresponds to a slump of 100-175 mm.

questions.question: How many layers of concrete are placed in the cone while performing the test?

explanations.a: Incorrect. The standard procedure requires 3 layers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The standard procedure requires 3 layers.
explanations.c: Correct! The mould is filled with concrete in 3 layers, each one-third of the height.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The standard procedure requires 3 layers.

questions.question: Slump value is measured in mm as-

answers.a: Difference of height of inverted cone and the height of mid portion of slump obtained
answers.b: Difference of height of inverted cone and the height of slump obtained measured from bottom
answers.c: Difference of height of inverted cone and height of 1/3 rd portion of slump from bottom
answers.d: Difference of mid height of inverted cone and height of slump obtained from bottom.

explanations.a: Incorrect. The measurement is taken from the highest point of the slump, not the mid portion.
explanations.b: Correct! The slump is measured as the difference between the height of the inverted cone and the height of the slump obtained measured from the bottom.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The measurement is not taken from the 1/3rd portion.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The full height of the inverted cone is used, not the mid height.

questions.question: Calculate the slump value in inches if height of mid point of slump so formed from bottom is 7.5 cm & based on this value, report the degree of workability.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Check the calculation: slump = 30 - 7.5 = 22.5 cm ≈ 9 inches, but degree of workability needs to be verified.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The calculated slump value does not equal 5 inches.
explanations.c: Incorrect. While the slump calculation may be close, verify the degree of workability.
explanations.d: Correct! The slump value calculates to approximately 6 inches, which falls in the High Workability range (4-7 inches).

questions.question: Sample I of controlled concrete has w/c ratio 0.25 and Sample II has w/c 0.45, among the two what do you think, which one can have comparatively higher workability?

answers.a: Sample I has higher workability than Sample II
answers.b: Sample II has higher workability than Sample I
answers.c: Sample I and II will have nearly same workability if all conditions kept constant as workability is independent of w/c ratio.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Lower w/c ratio leads to lower workability.
explanations.b: Correct! Higher w/c ratio (0.45) leads to higher workability compared to lower w/c ratio (0.25).
explanations.c: Incorrect. Workability is directly dependent on the w/c ratio.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The relationship between w/c ratio and workability is well established.

questions.question: What is the type of slump indicated by the figure shown (concrete spreads out significantly with no definite shape)?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Shear slump shows the concrete shearing off laterally from one side.
explanations.b: Correct! Collapsed slump indicates that the concrete mix is too wet and the mix is regarded as harsh and lean.
explanations.c: Incorrect. True slump shows a general even drop of concrete mass all around.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Zero slump means the concrete maintains its shape with no slump.
